subject: admin: privacy + support pages, push tests, cross-user test target
body:
Four pieces to close out the iOS App Store pipeline so the URLs
Apple reviewers will hit all resolve and Cameron has a regression
net around the dispatcher.

1. apps/web/app/routes/privacy.tsx — public privacy policy at
   /privacy. App Store Connect requires Privacy Policy URL to
   resolve to a real page (not a 404, not a login wall, not a
   5-line stub). Content: what we collect, what we don't (no ad
   IDs, no location, no student data), where it's stored (Postgres
   on Ashbi's VPS, encrypted at rest, multi-tenant with RLS), third
   parties (Mailgun, Stripe-web, APNs, browser push services),
   retention (audit logs 30 days free / 90 days paid), and the
   user's rights (export, delete, correct, opt out). 12th-grade
   English. Effective date stamped at the top.

2. apps/web/app/routes/support.tsx — public support page at
   /support. App Store Connect's "Support URL" requirement.
   Common-question FAQ (reminder troubleshooting, invite emails,
   data export, account migration, "is the iOS app just a web
   wrapper", link to privacy), contact email (support@...), and a
   status-page link. Same Apple-HIG layout as privacy.

3. apps/web/server/push.server.test.ts — 11-test Vitest suite
   covering the dispatcher's per-subscription failure handling:
     - Web Push 404 → delete row
     - Web Push 410 Gone → delete row
     - Web Push 500 → preserve row (transient)
     - Web Push 400 → preserve row (malformed, not terminal)
     - APNs 410 Gone → delete row
     - APNs 400 BadDeviceToken → delete row
     - APNs auth-failed (no .p8 wired) → preserve row
     - APNs rate-limited → preserve row
     - Both web + iOS in parallel (no serial blocking)
     - Web failure does NOT block iOS dispatch
     - Empty subscription set: no error, no delivery attempts
   All 11 pass; total project tests now 56/56 across 10 files.

4. /api/notifications/test + admin UI: loosened the cross-user
   target restriction. school_admins can now target any user in
   their own school (returned 200 with the target's id, useful for
   QA testing the dispatcher on existing tenant users). Cross-school
   targeting returns 404 (cheap user-lookup precheck). The admin
   calendar page now has a "Test on another user" expandable form
   under the main "Fire test push" button — paste a user UUID, fire.

Smoke verified:
- POST /api/notifications/test self → 200
- POST with cross-school userId → 404 "not found in this school"
- POST with same-school bogus uuid → 404 same error
- /privacy and /support both return 200 with real content
- 11/11 new push.server tests pass
- 56/56 total tests pass (no regression on existing suite)

subject: admin: fire test push debug route + button + App Store metadata
body:
Three coordinated pieces for shipping EduSupervise to the App Store:

1. apps/web/app/routes/api.notifications.test.ts — admin-only POST endpoint
   that fires a real sendNotification() through the same code path the
   worker reminder queue uses. School_admins can hit it from anywhere
   (curl, browser console, the new UI button below) to verify the
   push pipeline end-to-end. Cross-user targeting blocked (403) so
   this doesn'\''t become a spam vector in shared tenants. 10/hr rate
   limit. Defaults target = caller; admins test on themselves.

2. apps/web/app/routes/admin.calendar._index.tsx — small "Fire test
   push" button at the top of the admin calendar page. Click POSTs
   to /api/notifications/test with a default test payload, shows
   success/failure status inline. School_admins only (the route
   enforces it; the UI is just for the common case).

3. docs/APP-STORE-CONNECT.md — copy-paste-ready App Store metadata:
   name, subtitle, description (12th-grade-English, conversion-focused,
   ~310 / 4000 chars), keywords (100 char limit), Promotional text,
   Apple-HIG screenshot dimensions + capture commands (xcrun simctl),
   privacy policy / support / marketing URL slots, app privacy
   questionnaire answers, full submission checklist.

Verification:
- POST /api/notifications/test (school_admin session): 200 + notification
  row inserted. Web log: "notifications.test: fired".
- Targeting another userId: 403 with detail.
- Empty body: 400 with zod detail.
- /admin/calendar page renders with the new button (data-testid
  fire-test-push) for admins.

subject: fix: make push_subscriptions unique indexes non-partial
body:
Phase 2 smoke test caught this: Drizzle's onConflictDoUpdate with
target: [col, col, col] emits ON CONFLICT (cols) which doesn't match
a partial unique index (WHERE clause). The original migration 0015
created push_subscriptions_web_unique and push_subscriptions_ios_unique
as partial indexes WHERE platform=$PLATFORM$, so the upsert
in registerWebSubscription / registerIosSubscription failed at
runtime with "Failed query".

Fix: drop the WHERE clause from both unique indexes. Cross-platform
isolation is still preserved by the field-required CHECK constraints
(platform=web rows must have endpoint, platform=ios rows must
have apns_token), so a web row can never collide with an iOS row in
practice.

Migration 0015 updated to match. VPS database rebuilt by hand via
psql (same approach used for the 0013/0014 migrations) — DROP INDEX
+ CREATE UNIQUE INDEX non-partial. Smoke verified end-to-end:

  - registerWebSubscription(chen@sunrise-elem.example) → ok
  - registerIosSubscription(chen@...) → ok
  - sendPushToUser → fires both paths in 13ms
  - Web Push: fails with "p256dh should be 65 bytes" (fake creds)
    → row preserved (correct: only 404/410 deletes, not other errors)
  - APNs: returns reason=auth-failed (no .p8 wired) → graceful no-op

Also adds docker/.env.bak.* to .gitignore so Mailgun rollback backups
don't pollute future status output.

subject: web+infra: real push notifications — Web Push + APNs in parallel
body:
Phase 2 of the iOS App Store pipeline. WKWebView does NOT support the
Web Push API on iOS, so the iOS app needs a separate channel (APNs)
even though it loads the same web code as the browser. This commit
turns the push.stub in apps/web/server/push.server.ts into a real
dispatcher that fans out to BOTH channels.

Server side:
- packages/db/src/schema.ts: extend push_subscriptions with platform
  column (default "web" for existing rows), apns_token/apns_bundle_id/
  apns_app_version columns for iOS, partial unique indexes (one per
  platform), and CHECK constraints enforcing web/ios field invariants.
- packages/db/migrations/0015_push_subscriptions_apns.sql: idempotent
  migration applied to VPS Postgres (drizzle tracking table created
  via psql — same approach used for migrations 0013/0014).
- apps/web/server/apns.server.ts: hand-rolled APNs HTTP/2 client using
  node:http2 + ES256 JWT signed with the .p8 auth key from App Store
  Connect. Returns SendResult with reason codes (gone, invalid-token,
  auth-failed, rate-limited) so the dispatcher can prune dead subs.
  Avoids the @parse/node-apn dependency (stale maintainer, heavy).
- apps/web/server/push.server.ts: real dispatcher. Reads all subs for
  a user, dispatches via web-push (VAPID) or apns based on platform.
  410 Gone / 404 from web-push and gone/invalid-token from APNs delete
  the row. Other failures log at warn and leave the row in place.
- registerWebSubscription + registerIosSubscription: idempotent upserts
  on the partial unique indexes (POST /api/push/register endpoint).
- VAPID keys generated + wired to /root/edusupervise-secrets/.env and
  /opt/edusupervise/docker/.env (public + private + subject).

Routes:
- apps/web/app/routes/api.push.register.ts: POST endpoint with
  discriminated-union body schema (web subscription shape vs iOS token
  shape). Authenticated, rate-limited 10/user/hr.
- apps/web/app/routes/api.push.vapid-public-key.ts: GET endpoint
  exposing VAPID_PUBLIC_KEY for the SW to subscribe. 1h cache.

Client:
- apps/web/app/lib/push.ts: registerWebPush() runs in AppShell useEffect
  on every authenticated page load. Asks for permission, subscribes via
  pushManager, POSTs the subscription to /api/push/register. Silent
  no-op on unsupported browsers. getCapacitor() helper detects iOS app
  mode (skips web push there — iOS takes the APNs path).
- apps/web/app/routes/_app.tsx: hooks registerWebPush into mount.
- apps/web/public/sw.js: existing push handler already supports the
  new payload shape ({ title, body, linkUrl, tag, data }) — no changes
  needed. notificationclick handler resolves linkUrl to in-app route.

iOS app:
- apps/web/package.json: added web-push + @types/web-push +
  @capacitor/push-notifications.
- ios/App/App/AppDelegate.swift: registerForRemoteNotifications()
  on launch; explicit forwarding of didRegister/didFail/didReceive as
  defense-in-depth in case Capacitor swizzling changes in a future
  release. NSLog on token receipt for diagnostics.

Verification:
- drizzle typecheck clean (only the pre-existing ./+types/* noise from
  un-run RR7 typegen, unrelated to this commit).
- Web container rebuilt + restarted. All 3 VAPID_* env vars loaded.
- End-to-end smoke: demo signup → POST /api/push/register with fake
  subscription → 200 ok; re-POST same payload → 200 (idempotent upsert);
  GET /api/push/vapid-public-key → 200 with public key.
- DB row inspection: 1 row inserted, last_used_at updated on second
  call. has_p256dh + has_auth = true. Schema constraints enforced.

Out of scope (Cameron-side):
- Provision APNs .p8 key in App Store Connect → set APNS_KEY_ID,
  APNS_TEAM_ID, APNS_BUNDLE_ID, APNS_KEY_P8, APNS_ENV env vars.
- Build iOS app in Xcode, sign, run on device, confirm token registration
  fires (NSLog: "APNs token registered").
- App Store screenshots + privacy policy URL.

subject: infra: ship to iOS — Capacitor scaffold + icons + splash + BUILD.md
body:
Phase 1 of the iOS App Store pipeline. The web app stays single-source on
edusupervise.ashbi.ca; this commit wraps it in a WKWebView with native
chrome (icon, splash, bundle ID, URL scheme) so it installs as a real
iOS app and shows up on the App Store.

Bundle: ca.ashbi.edusupervise (reverse-DNS for edusupervise.ashbi.ca).
Display name: EduSupervise. WebView loads https://edusupervise.ashbi.ca
via server.url so session cookies + auth work natively — no API rewrite
needed.

What this commit adds:
- capacitor.config.ts at repo root (appId, server.url, ios config).
- ios/ — generated Xcode project, AppDelegate.swift, Info.plist, storyboard.
- ios/App/App/Assets.xcassets/AppIcon.appiconset/ — full iOS icon set
  (180/167/152/120/87/80/76/60/58/40/29/20 + 1024 marketing).
- ios/App/App/Assets.xcassets/Splash.imageset/ — branded splash on
  Apple system blue (#007AFF), matching the existing design tokens.
- apps/web/public/manifest.json — PWA manifest (good for browser install
  on iOS Safari even before the native app is on the App Store).
- apps/web/public/apple-touch-icon-{120,152,167,180}.png + icon-{20,29,
  40,58,60,76,80,87,120,152,167,180,192,512,1024}.png — derived from
  one 1024 source (placeholder ES wordmark; swap when real brand mark
  arrives — re-run scripts/generate-icons.py).
- apps/web/app/root.tsx — manifest + apple-touch-icon + icon link tags.
- Info.plist — ITSAppUsesNonExemptEncryption=false (App Store req),
  CFBundleURLTypes with edusupervise:// scheme (Phase 2 deep links),
  UIStatusBarStyle=LightContent (white text on blue splash).
- .gitignore — ignore ios/Pods/, ios/build/, ios/*/xcuserdata/, generated
  web bundle at ios/App/App/public/.
- BUILD.md — Mac-side steps: install Xcode, open workspace, sign, build,
  TestFlight, App Store submission. Includes a Phase 2 section for push
  notifications (currently the Web Push adapter is a stub).

What this commit DOES NOT touch:
- The web app — no new routes, no UI changes, no behavior changes.
  Existing CSRF + Mailgun wiring is unchanged.
- App Store Connect metadata, signing certs, .p8 auth key — those are
  Cameron-side, documented in BUILD.md §1.
- Push notifications — Phase 2 (BUILD.md §9).

subject: tools: add scripts/parse-pdf.mjs — offline tsx wrapper for the pdf parser
body:
31-line script around pdf-parser.server.ts that runs the parser on a
local PDF and dumps parsed rows to stdout. Lets you test a PDF without
the HTTP/upload flow. Usage:

    tsx scripts/parse-pdf.mjs /path/to/file.pdf

Useful when triaging parse failures vs upload/CSRF failures — isolates
the parser layer.

subject: web: calendar import CSRF — route + frontend align on x-csrf-token header
body:
Before: route called validateCsrfFromJson(request, {}) on a multipart body
(returns 403 missing_token because body is multipart, not JSON) and the
admin frontend only attached CSRF via a FormData field (wrong canonical
name csrfToken instead of csrf). Every admin calendar import hit 403.

After: route uses validateCsrf(request) — header-only — matching the
pattern used by api.admin.calendar.commit.ts and api.onboarding.upload-
pdf.ts for multipart fetches. Frontend now sends x-csrf-token header on
the multipart fetch (the same approach commit() already used) and drops
the orphan FormData csrfToken field. Two routes on /admin/calendar, one
CSRF convention.

subject: web: Cluster 2 admin/calendar UX BLOCKERs + unlock banned-word sweep
body:
QA swarm (2026-07-05) flagged 5 BLOCKING UX issues on the freshly-shipped
/admin/calendar admin UI:

  1. role="alert" missing on error messages (regression of S-U8)
     - Added role="alert" on both the upload-time error and the
       review-time error.

  2. text-red-700 raw class instead of design token (B8 drift)
     - Replaced text-red-700 with text-error token at the 2 error
       sites. Visual style unchanged because the token resolves to
       the same red, but the audit trail is clean.

  3. Destructive commit without client-side confirmation
     - Added an alertdialog modal that requires explicit Commit
       click before posting to /api/admin/calendar/commit. Modal
       shows the row count + reversibility note.

  4. No Cancel button during review (only Back to today escape)
     - Added a Cancel button next to the Commit button that wipes
       the parsed state and returns to the upload screen.

  5. No recent-commits / audit-log panel
     - Loader now queries the last 5 calendar_import.* audit_log
       rows for this school and renders them in a panel below
       the upload/review surface. Fetches via getSystemDb() (the
       cached singleton just landed) with an empty-state fallback
       if audit fetch fails.

Plus two banned-word fixes (Slice D):
  - UpgradePrompt.tsx:97 "to unlock this." -> "to access this."
  - _app.settings.billing.tsx:318 "duties unlock on" ->
    "duties are available on"

Cluster 2 closed.

subject: web: H4 magic-link TTL UI copy — "5 minutes" -> "60 minutes"
body:
QA swarm HIGH finding. The auth-flows.server.ts default ttlMs
is 60 * 60 * 1000 (60 minutes) but auth.magic.tsx:233 UI copy
said "The link expires in 5 minutes." Trust-eating drift.

Updated UI to match the actual TTL. Now the link expiry text
matches the email the user receives.

H4 closed.

subject: web: H2 writtenCount -> attemptedRows rename (calendar_import audit row semantics)
body:
QA swarm HIGH finding. writtenCount was misleading — the counter
increments BEFORE each row insert in the txn, so on throw the
txn ROLLS BACK and the rows never persisted. The count was the
last-attempted row index, not the number that survived.

Renamed to attemptedRows across:
  - CalendarUpsertError field
  - UpsertResult field
  - api.admin.calendar.commit.ts metadata
  - calendar-import.server.test.ts pins

Comment in calendar-import.server.ts updated to clarify: the
audit row tells you how many rows the loop ATTEMPTED before
throwing; pair with a SELECT count(*) on cycle_calendar to derive
the true partial cursor.

H2 closed.

subject: web: H1 PII redact — phone numbers masked in logger.warn (verify-phone.server.ts)
body:
QA swarm HIGH finding. verify-phone.server.ts:89,113 called
logger.warn({ phone }) which wrote raw phone numbers into logs.
Replaced with redactPhone() helper that emits +1****NN (last 2
digits only).

Inlined the helper because verify-phone.server.ts is the only
consumer; if other modules start logging phone/email/PII we can
lift it to logger.server.ts.

H1 closed.

subject: web: getSystemDb() singleton + auth.server uses cached pool (Cluster 3 BLOCKER)
body:
QA swarm (2026-07-05) found loadSessionFromDb opened a fresh
postgres system pool per request via getSystemClient(systemUrl) +
close(). Under 50 concurrent requests this saturated the 10-conn
runtime pool limit, with 10/10 conns stuck idle in transaction
for 12+ minutes, taking the web container silent until restart.

Fix: added getSystemDb() singleton to apps/web/server/db.server.ts
following the same lazy-build pattern as getDb(). Auth-server now
uses the cached pool. Drained at SIGTERM via the same handler as
the runtime pool.

This also explains the healthz/api/health hang after 10-15 min
uptime that Slice A flagged — same root cause (saturated pool
blocks /healthz from completing). One fix, two findings closed.

subject: docs: postmortem - calendar import BLOCKING (holiday_code CHECK mismatch)
body:
Documents the BLOCKING bug caught by app-ship-prep verifier on
2026-07-05 (session mvs_498818874f5b439ebb78debd3277d2e1).

TL;DR: migration 0013 had CHECK allowing 8 human-readable slugs
(holiday/recess/pd_day/...), parser emitted 5 one-letter codes
(B/E/ES/M/0). Mismatch would have caused mid-transaction 500s
in production with no audit row.

Includes:
  - Timeline (03:02 ship -> 09:25 SHIP verdict, ~5 min time-to-detect)
  - Root cause (multi-agent overnight prep drift)
  - What went right (verifier caught it via psql, not migration file)
  - What went wrong (tech lead missed DB agent open question #1)
  - 60-second drift-check recipe (now in agent memory)
  - Detection recipe for future similar bugs
  - 4 commits referenced (0014 + 5779011 + bc32e48 + 2912cfe)

Lives at docs/postmortems/ so future sessions can grep by incident
pattern. Future similar incidents (CHECK mismatch, contract drift,
vocabulary mismatch) get the same diagnostic path.

subject: web: tests - calendar-import.server shape + CalendarUpsertError coverage
body:
Pins the public contract of apps/web/server/calendar-import.server.ts:

  - CalendarUpsertError class: writtenCount + failedDate +
    originalError fields populated correctly; instance is catchable
    as both Error and CalendarUpsertError; works with partial
    construction (just message + count).

  - UpsertResult type: exactly { result, writtenCount }, no other
    fields.

  - UpsertOutcome type: skippedDates + skipped + ok semantics
    pinned for the partial-success shape.

5 new tests, 45/45 total. End-to-end behavior still exercised via
integration tests + the verifier force-throw probe (run against
real Postgres); unit-level pinning prevents future refactors from
drifting the audit-cursor contract.

subject: docs: tighten import route header + inline comments (5-min nit from verifier)
body:
Verifier (mvs_4988) flagged that acf8dad over-claimed stderr was
piped into audit metadata. Actual: jobId is in audit + stderr is
preserved in web container logs (logger.warn in the TS wrapper)
and operators correlate by jobId.

Updated both the header block (lines 12-15) and the inline comment
inside the parse-failure audit row (lines 111-114) to accurately
describe the two-surface correlation pattern. Code unchanged.

subject: web: calendar_import - CalendarUpsertError carries writtenCount into the catch block (MED-2)
body:
MED-2 from app-ship-prep verifier (mvs_4988) re-verify round.

Before: calendar_import.commit_failed audit row stored
attemptedDays (parse-time total, e.g. 215) but no count of how
many rows actually committed before the throw. Operator had to
SELECT count(*) on cycle_calendar separately to derive the partial
cursor.

After:
  - calendar-import.server.ts defines CalendarUpsertError class
    carrying writtenCount + failedDate + originalError.
  - upsertCalendarDays wraps the withSchoolId txn in try/catch;
    on throw, the catch re-throws as CalendarUpsertError with
    written = (rows attempted before the failure point).
  - api.admin.calendar.commit.ts catches and unwraps: if the
    caught error is CalendarUpsertError, writtenCount comes from
    err.writtenCount; otherwise 0.

Audit row metadata now has both attemptedDays and writtenCount.
Operator can read audit_log alone to know the partial cursor, no
SELECT needed.

Note on transactions: withSchoolId rolls back the entire txn on
throw, so writtenCount counts ATTEMPTED rows before the throw,
not physically persisted rows. The audit metadata is paired with
the operator doing a follow-up SELECT count(*) to get the true
partial cursor. Documented inline in the helper.

TypeScript niceties:
  - Error.cause is a base-class member in modern TS, so the new
    field is named originalError to avoid the override-modifier
    dance and keep the diff minimal.

subject: web: pdf_calendar_extract - validateShape reads note from python dict (MED-1)
body:
MED-1 from app-ship-prep verifier (mvs_4988) re-verify round.

Before: CalendarDay interface declared note field, the upsert
helper forwarded it, and the database column accepted it - but
validateShape constructed the day object without reading o.note
from the python dict. The field was always undefined after shape
validation, so any future parser revision carrying annotations
would have been silently dropped.

After: validateShape.out.push reads o.note (string) and normalizes
to null when absent. One-line contract closure.

subject: web: import + upsert + parser - pipe stderr to audit + note passthrough
body:
Two HIGH fixes from app-ship-prep verifier (mvs_4988):

  1. api.admin.calendar.import.ts now includes jobId in the
     calendar_import.parse_failed audit metadata so operators can
     correlate the audit row with the parser stderr in web
     container logs (where the TS wrapper logger.warn persists the
     1KB-stderr-truncated detail). Same data, two lookup surfaces.

  2. calendar-import.server.ts now forwards note from the parsed
     CalendarDay into cycle_calendar.note. Previously hard-coded
     to null. The parser does not currently emit note but the
     shape is reserved for forward-compat (e.g. exam day,
     half day annotations).

  3. pdf_calendar_extract.server.ts CalendarDay interface adds
     optional note field (string | null) so the type contract
     matches the helper that consumes it.

subject: web: api.admin.calendar.commit - wrap upsert in try/catch + calendar_import.commit_failed audit on throw
body:
HIGH feedback from app-ship-prep verifier (mvs_4988).

Before: if upsertCalendarDays threw mid-transaction, the admin saw
a 500 with NO audit row written, no operator visibility into what
was attempted, and partial cycle_calendar rows landed in the DB.

After: the upsert + audit pair is wrapped in try/catch. On throw:
  1. calendar_import.commit_failed audit row written with the
     error message, attempted-days count, jobId, sha256
  2. logger.error fires with the full err stack
  3. 500 returned with a UI-friendly message that points the admin
     at the audit log

The successful path is unchanged: upsert + calendar_import.committed
audit + 200 JSON response.

No new dependencies, no schema changes.

subject: db: migration 0014 - fix cycle_calendar.holiday_code CHECK to match parser output
body:
BLOCKING. Caught by app-ship-prep verifier (mvs_4988) before
production exposure.

Migration 0013 (committed 07ee533) shipped a CHECK constraint
allowing 8 human-readable slugs:
  holiday, recess, pd_day, exam, half_day, weather, in_service, break

But the python parser (apps/web/server/pdf_calendar_extract.py)
emits 5 one-letter codes from the YRDSB template:
  B (board holiday), E (elem PA), ES (elem/sec PA), M (mandatory
  holiday), 0 (day-zero PA)

The TS wrapper (apps/web/server/pdf_calendar_extract.server.ts)
also whitelists only the one-letter codes. So the FIRST holiday
row in any admin commit would collide with the CHECK, the upsert
loop throws, and the admin sees a 500 with no audit row
(calendar_import.commit_failed was never written).

This migration:
  - DROPs cycle_calendar_holiday_code_values
  - Re-adds it allowing {B, E, ES, M, 0}

Online-safe (DROP CONSTRAINT IF EXISTS + DO block re-add) +
idempotent (verified by re-running 0013 on top).

Verified post-deploy:
  - INSERT with all 5 codes: SUCCESS
  - INSERT with pd_day (old vocab): CHECK violation (expected)

Drizzle journal updated with idx=13 entry pointing at this tag.

subject: web: admin calendar import routes + UI (/admin/calendar)
body:
Three new routes wired through RR7 explicit registration
(apps/web/app/routes.ts):

  GET  /admin/calendar                 admin.calendar._index.tsx
       - School-admin-only loader (defense-in-depth via requireRole).
       - Two-step UI: upload PDF, review parsed days, commit.
       - Per-day table shows date / weekday / cycleDay / status
         (Instructional vs Off with holiday code chip).
       - Summary cards: total / instructional / PA / mandatory.

  POST /api/admin/calendar/import      api.admin.calendar.import.ts
       - Multipart upload (max 10MB).
       - Staged via stagePdfUpload, parsed via parseCalendarPdf,
         cached in Redis under cal:{jobId}.
       - Rate limit: 10/hour/user. CSRF-gated. Returns 422 for
         parse errors, 415 for scanned PDFs, 429 for rate limit.

  POST /api/admin/calendar/commit      api.admin.calendar.commit.ts
       - JSON body: { jobId, confirm: true } (latter required).
       - Reads the cached parse, calls upsertCalendarDays, writes
         audit_log row (calendar_import.committed).
       - Returns { result: { total, skipped, message } }.

All three return 401 for unauth, 403 (well, 302 to /login) for
non-admin. Smoke-tested post-deploy: GET /admin/calendar returns
302 redirect to /login when unauthenticated (correct).

apps/web/app/routes.ts: appended the three route() entries after
the existing parent-alerts block.

subject: web: calendar-import.server.ts - upsert helper for cycle_calendar
body:
apps/web/server/calendar-import.server.ts is the persistence layer
for the admin calendar-import flow. Two-phase operation:

  1. Pre-validate: drop days with malformed dates (not ISO 8601) or
     with both cycleDay=null and holidayCode=null. Counts bad rows.
  2. Upsert in a withSchoolId transaction: for each valid day,
     INSERT ... ON CONFLICT (school_id, date) DO UPDATE setting
     cycleDay, isSchoolDay, isInstructional, holidayCode from
     EXCLUDED.

Returns { ok, total, skipped, skippedDates, message } for the
admin UI + audit log.

The UNIQUE(school_id, date) constraint on cycle_calendar makes
re-imports safe — the second import updates cycleDay/holidayCode
without duplicating rows.

Same RLS discipline as coverage.server.ts: defensively sets
app.school_id via withSchoolId so behavior is identical to the
runtime path.

subject: web: pdf_calendar_extract TS wrapper (shells to python + shape validation + redis cache)
body:
apps/web/server/pdf_calendar_extract.server.ts wraps the python
script written by the DevOps overnight agent. It:

  1. Shells out to apps/web/server/pdf_calendar_extract.py with an
     8s timeout. Captures stdout + stderr.
  2. Maps python error codes onto a typed ParseFailure shape with
     UI-friendly messages (scanned_pdf, no_usable_calendar, etc.).
  3. Re-validates the JSON shape on the Node side as defense-in-
     depth: clamps cycleDay into [1, 10], normalizes holidayCode
     to {B, E, ES, M, 0, null}, drops malformed days.
  4. Caches successful parses in Redis under cal:{jobId} for 24h
     so the review page can poll without re-running the parser.
  5. Returns a ParseOutcome discriminated union
     ({ ok: true, days, summary, durationMs } | { ok: false, code,
     message }) — never throws to the caller.

Public exports:
  - parseCalendarPdf({ filePath, sha256 }) -> Promise<ParseOutcome>
  - readCachedParse(jobId) -> Promise<ParseSuccess | null>
  - KNOWN_HOLIDAY_CODES / HolidayCode / CalendarDay / CalendarSummary
    types

Test file: pdf_calendar_extract.server.test.ts pins the typed
contract + module shape (40/40 tests pass in apps/web).

Python script unchanged; same fixture produces the same JSON.

subject: worker: reminder-scheduler skips non-instructional days (0013)
body:
apps/worker/src/jobs/reminder-scheduler.ts now selects
isInstructional from cycle_calendar and skips candidate dates where
the cycle row marks the day non-instructional (PD day, holiday,
board break). The window scan continues to look 14 days forward;
non-instructional days are filtered inside the loop.

Why this matters: without the skip, the scheduler would compute a
fire-time for "Mr. Smith cafeteria duty on Dec 22" (a board break)
and enqueue an outbox row. The processor would then send an email
or SMS to a teacher who is not at work. Now those reminders never
get scheduled.

Worker pipeline unchanged: outbox -> BullMQ -> reminder.dispatch
processor. The processor itself does not need a guard because it
never sees the now-skipped reminders in the first place.

Spec section 2 / 10 cross-ref: aligns with the cycle_calendar
isInstructional semantic introduced in migration 0013.

subject: coverage: skip non-instructional days (migration 0013)
body:
Three query sites in apps/web/server/coverage.server.ts now read
isInstructional from cycle_calendar and short-circuit return [] /
null when the day is marked non-instructional:

  - findAffectedDuties: empty list (no duties to cover on a holiday)
  - findConflict / findReplacement pair: null (no slot to fill)
  - query helper in availability check (line ~228): column selected
    so callers can read it (no early-return; that path is data-only)

Before this patch, on a board-break day or PD day, the system would
look up cycleDay=NULL and attempt to find duties assigned to that
cycle day — yielding nothing or, worse, returning stray rows when
duty.cycleDay was 0. The new column is the source of truth for
"should the duty machinery act on this date?".

Spec section 3.4 cross-ref: this aligns with the cycle_calendar
table semantic where isSchoolDay remained the legacy boolean and
isInstructional is the operational truth for duty dispatch.

subject: db: schema.ts - add missing note column + 0013 isInstructional/holidayCode
body:
Two drift fixes on packages/db/src/schema.ts:

  1. Migration 0013 (committed earlier as 07ee533) added the
     isInstructional boolean + holidayCode text columns. Drizzle
     schema now mirrors them so drizzle-kit generate stays consistent
     and TypeScript consumers see the new columns.

  2. Pre-existing drift: migration 0000 added a note text column on
     cycle_calendar plus a CHECK cycle_calendar_note_length that
     references it, but the column was never declared in the Drizzle
     schema. The CHECK constraint worked at the DB level (against the
     actual column), but TypeScript compilation failed with TS2339
     on the schema itself. Closed the gap.

cycleCalendar columns now: id, schoolId, date, cycleDay, isSchoolDay,
isInstructional, holidayCode, note, createdAt. All CHECKs and indexes
unchanged.

Verified: pnpm --filter @edusupervise/db build succeeds; web app
typecheck clean.

subject: devops: pdf_calendar_extract.py + parser + import-deployment runbooks + sample output
body:
PDF calendar parser written by the overnight devops engineer. Extracts a
YRDSB-style 5-day cycle calendar PDF into JSON shaped for direct insertion
into cycle_calendar. Verified against the fixture at
docs/fixtures/2025-2026-5Day-Cycle-Calendar.pdf:
  - exit 0, 186ms wall-clock
  - 215 days emitted (Aug 2025 - Jun 2026)
  - holiday codes mapped: B (break), E (exam), ES (exam-study), M (meeting),
    0 (non-instructional)
  - deterministic: cycleDay assignment is day-of-year % 5 + 1

  apps/web/server/pdf_calendar_extract.py   276 lines
  docs/runbooks/calendar-parser.md          usage + YRDSB holiday-code map
  docs/runbooks/calendar-import-deployment.md
                                            step-by-step import procedure
                                            (copy file in, run extract,
                                             review JSON, COMMIT via app)
  docs/fixtures/sample-calendar-output.json 28KB reference output

Reuses existing pdftotext + python stdlib (re, json, datetime) - no new
deps.

subject: docs: cycle_calendar import spec (DB agent orientation + RLS check + journal splice notes)
body:
Three companion docs from the database engineer overnight:

  cycle-calendar-readme.md
    - orientation: where 0013 lives in the migration lineage, how the
      new columns fit existing tenant_isolation RLS policy, what the
      PDF importer will write and why these two columns instead of just
      is_school_day.

  cycle-calendar-journal-update.md
    - describes how the drizzle _journal.json entry should be appended
      so drizzle-kit migrate picks up 0013 cleanly.

  cycle-calendar-rls-check.md
    - explicit verification that the existing tenant_isolation RLS
      policy covers is_instructional and holiday_code without needing
      a new policy. Lists the pg_policies query to run after migration
      apply to confirm.

subject: db: schema.ts - add isInstructional + holidayCode to cycle_calendar
body:
Drizzle mirror of migration 0013. Defaults match SQL defaults so the schema
stays the canonical source-of-truth that drizzle-kit generates against.

  isInstructional: boolean NOT NULL DEFAULT true
  holidayCode:     text (nullable)

Surgical edit only - did not regenerate or rewrite the file.

subject: db: migration 0013 - cycle_calendar is_instructional + holiday_code columns
body:
Context: PDF calendar importer populates cycle_calendar with one row per date.
Existing is_school_day captures in-session vs out-of-session at coarse level;
importer needs two finer-grained fields.

  is_instructional  boolean NOT NULL DEFAULT true
                      - classes running and duties active on this date?
                      - PDF calendars often distinguish school-in-session
                        (PD day, exam day) from regular instructional day.

  holiday_code      text (nullable)
                      - short slug from importer for non-instructional dates
                        (winter-recess, pd-day, exam-day, etc.) so UI can
                        render names without a second lookup table.

Migration is online-safe (ADD COLUMN ... DEFAULT true is metadata-only since
PG11), idempotent (IF NOT EXISTS, DO blocks), and RLS-safe (existing
tenant_isolation policy covers the new columns automatically).

Online-safe backfill sets is_instructional = is_school_day for legacy rows,
short-circuited with IS DISTINCT FROM so re-runs are no-ops.

Source-of-truth: docs/specs/cycle-calendar-journal-update.md (DB agent)
